metadata.title: Why the 90-Second Request Takes 12 Minutes and the 8-Second Request Takes 45

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Hola, Liam — in for Bear. Teacher B spends twelve minutes total? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Teacher B spends twelve minutes total?

beats.narration_text: Teacher A types eight words. Teacher B types ninety words and spends ninety seconds. Teacher A spends forty-five minutes fixing what Claude got wrong. Teacher B spends twelve minutes total.

card.copy: Why the 90-second request takes 12 minutes and the 8-second one takes 45

beats.narration_text: The eight-word request: Add a syllabus page to my class website. Claude produces a page. The page has a JavaScript-rendered table, a CDN-loaded font, inline styles. The school server blocks the CDN. The teacher spends forty-five minutes removing the JavaScript and the external fonts.

production_viz.label: 8-word prompt — Claude's defaults — 45 minutes of correction
production_viz.mechanic: left: the 8-word prompt in a terminal card; right: a crimson list of Claude's defaults — JavaScript table, CDN font, inline styles; a crimson label '45 min to fix'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] request prompt and its crimson defaults

beats.narration_text: The ninety-second version names the file, names what must not change, restates the relevant CLAUDE.md rules, describes what done looks like, and lists what Claude must not do. Claude produces exactly that. Twelve minutes total.
beats.scene_description: a specification document with five labeled sections pinned to newsprint, editorial collage style, desaturated
beats.image_prompt: B03, printed five-section specification document — Operation, Invariants, Context, Output format, Negative constraint — typed in five labeled rows, pinned to aged newsprint, editorial collage style, highly desaturated, cream and charcoal tones
beats.new_visual_element: [STILL ai] five-element spec as printed document

beats.narration_text: Here is the question. A detailed request should take longer to write and produce comparable results. Here is the case where the detailed version finished thirty-three minutes faster. Why?

The request delegates four decisions to Claude. File path: Claude invents one. CSS approach: Claude defaults to what it was trained on — often Tailwind or inline styles. JavaScript: Claude defaults to included. External dependencies: Claude defaults to whatever is convenient. Each default is wrong for the class-website project. The forty-five minutes is four wrong decisions discovered and corrected one at a time.

production_viz.label: four scope decisions delegated to Claude's defaults
production_viz.mechanic: four rows appearing one at a time — file path, CSS approach, JavaScript, dependencies — each labeled 'Claude decides' in crimson with a wrong-X; a total label '45 min correction'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] four delegated decisions — each crimson wrong

beats.narration_text: The five-element specification is the format that reclaims all four decisions. Operation names the specific file. Invariants name what must not change. Context restates the CLAUDE.md rules that govern this step. Output format names what done looks like. Negative constraint names what Claude must not do. Each element is one of the four decisions — made by you in ninety seconds instead of by Claude and corrected at a cost of forty-five minutes.

production_viz.label: five elements — each claiming one of the four decisions
production_viz.mechanic: a five-row table drawing in teal — Operation, Invariants, Context, Output format, Negative constraint — each row appearing in sequence with a teal check; no crimson in this scene

beats.narration_text: The worked comparison. Request: Add a syllabus page. Specification: Create src/syllabus.html. No existing files modified. Per CLAUDE.md: vanilla HTML, no build step. Output: semantic table with units and dates, nav link added, renders at 375px. Negative constraint: no JavaScript, no CDN fonts, no new dependencies. Each specification cell removes one default from Claude's scope.

production_viz.label: request vs specification — two columns
production_viz.mechanic: two-column table: left 'Request' in crimson (generic or blank cells); right 'Specification' in teal (specific entries for each of the five rows); cells fill in alternating left/right

beats.narration_text: The ninety seconds is the decisions. The decisions need to be made in any case. The question is who makes them and when. The specification makes them at the start, in ninety seconds. The request defers them to Claude, which makes them wrong, and then the teacher makes them again under correction pressure — for forty-five minutes.

production_viz.label: 90 seconds now vs 45 minutes later — the same decisions
production_viz.mechanic: two timelines: left 'specification' — 90s bar (teal) + 12 min bar (teal); right 'request' — 8s bar (ink) + 45 min bar (crimson); a serif label 'same decisions, different moment'

beats.narration_text: Here is where this bites in the classroom. Teacher wants to add a Resources page to a class website that runs on a school server. Request: Add a resources page. Claude generates a JavaScript accordion with Google Fonts. School server blocks googleapis.com. Teacher discovers this 38 minutes into cleaning it up. A negative constraint — no JavaScript, no external fonts — would have prevented the failure in two words.

production_viz.label: the classroom case — negative constraint prevents 38 minutes
production_viz.mechanic: a two-step flow: left 'request -> CDN blocked (crimson X)'; right 'negative constraint: no CDN -> clean page (teal check)'; a time label '38 min saved'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] classroom case: CDN blocked vs negative constraint

beats.narration_text: The practical move: before any Claude Code task, write the five elements. Operation: the specific file. Invariants: what must not change. Context: the CLAUDE.md rules. Output format: what done looks like. Negative constraint: two or three things Claude must not do. First time: three to five minutes. Tenth time: sixty to ninety seconds. The format is the conducting work that makes the rest of the build fast.

production_viz.label: the five-element format — practical card
production_viz.mechanic: a teal card with five rows drawing in one by one: Operation / Invariants / Context / Output format / Negative constraint; a time note 'first time: 3-5 min; tenth time: 90 sec'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] practical five-element card with time note

beats.narration_text: The specification does not remove Claude's value. Claude still reads the project, executes the plan, iterates against its own checks. What the specification removes is Claude's authority to make scope decisions that belong to the teacher. The ninety seconds is not overhead. It is the conducting work.

card.copy: The decisions happen either way. The spec makes them yours.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why the 90-Second Request Takes 12 Minutes and the 8-Second Request Ta] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
